description_long: This machine gun features a long, cylindrical multi-barrel assembly reminiscent of a rotary or Gatling-style design, with visible barrel clusters and a robust, industrial appearance. The main body is constructed from dark, matte-finished metal, with a hexagonal or faceted grip section and reinforced barrel shroud. The rear section includes a prominent handle and what appears to be a mounting or support bracket, while the front end is dominated by the exposed barrels. There are no visible stock or shoulder supports, suggesting a design intended for mounting or vehicle integration rather than shoulder firing. The surface is primarily black with subtle metallic highlights and some brownish discoloration near the rear, indicating possible heat exposure or wear. The overall construction is solid and utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function.
description: A heavy, rotary-barrel machine gun with a dark, matte metal finish, featuring a multi-barrel cluster and robust grip. The design is industrial and utilitarian, with visible mounting hardware and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular arrangement of the multi-barrel cluster and the faceted grip below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the rotary barrel assembly, mounting bracket, and handle details.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the grip, handle, and the central axis of the barrel cluster.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the barrel assembly and mounting hardware.
attribute_barrel_type: rotary multi-barrel cluster with cylindrical profile
attribute_mounting_mechanism: mounting bracket integrated into the body
